title: Extreme Heat Warning: Rising Temperatures Put Millions at Risk - sebd.in

full_text:
An extreme heat warning has been issued across multiple regions as temperatures continue to rise to dangerous levels. Meteorological departments are urging residents to take immediate precautions as prolonged exposure to high temperatures can lead to serious health risks. Heatwaves are becoming more frequent due to changing climate patterns, making it essential for individuals to stay informed and prepared.
Authorities have emphasized that this is not just typical summer heat. The current conditions are classified as extreme, meaning temperatures are significantly higher than seasonal averages. This situation increases the risk of dehydration, heat exhaustion, and even life-threatening heatstroke, especially among vulnerable groups such as children, the elderly, and outdoor workers.
What Does an Extreme Heat Warning Mean?
An extreme heat warning is issued when temperatures are expected to reach levels that can pose a serious threat to human health. It typically indicates prolonged periods of high heat combined with high humidity, which makes it harder for the body to cool down naturally.
During such warnings, public health agencies recommend limiting outdoor activities, staying hydrated, and using cooling methods like fans or air conditioning. Ignoring these warnings can lead to severe health complications, particularly if precautions are not taken seriously.
Main Causes Behind Increasing Heatwaves
The rise in extreme heat events is closely linked to global climate changes. Increased greenhouse gas emissions have led to higher average global temperatures, making heatwaves more intense and longer-lasting.
Urbanization also plays a significant role. Cities tend to trap heat due to concrete structures and limited green spaces, a phenomenon known as the urban heat island effect. This results in even higher temperatures in densely populated areas compared to rural surroundings.
Health Risks Associated with Extreme Heat
Exposure to extreme heat can have serious health consequences. Common conditions include heat cramps, heat exhaustion, and heatstroke. Heatstroke is particularly dangerous and can lead to organ failure if not treated promptly.
Symptoms to watch for include dizziness, excessive sweating, nausea, rapid heartbeat, and confusion. In severe cases, individuals may lose consciousness. Immediate medical attention is required if heatstroke is suspected.
Who Is Most at Risk?
Certain groups are more vulnerable during an extreme heat warning. These include elderly individuals, infants, people with chronic illnesses, and those who work outdoors such as construction workers and farmers.
Additionally, individuals without access to proper cooling systems are at a higher risk. Socioeconomic factors can also play a role, as not everyone has equal access to resources like air conditioning or clean drinking water.
Essential Safety Tips During Extreme Heat
Staying safe during extreme heat requires proactive measures. One of the most important steps is to stay hydrated by drinking plenty of water throughout the day. Avoid beverages that can dehydrate the body, such as alcohol and caffeine.

title: CM’s Grievance Cell To Remain Closed On May 11 Due To Heatwave - Ommcom News

full_text:
Bhubaneswar: The Chief Minister’s Grievance Cell will remain closed on Monday (May 11, 2026) due to the prevailing intense heatwave conditions.
According to an official press release issued by the General Administration and Public Grievance Department, the grievance cell located at Unit-V in Bhubaneswar will remain closed on the said date in view of the severe heatwave affecting the region.
The department further informed that the date for the next hearing will be announced through newspapers and other media platforms.
